summary:
The provided evidence does not establish behavioral risk factors as the primary cause of the progression from optimal to hypertensive blood pressure in Black South Africans. Instead, it highlights shared physiological underpinnings involving conditions like metabolic syndrome without supporting specific attribution to behavioral causes within this demographic group. 

(Note: This summary is based strictly on what can be inferred directly from the text.)

Final Summary: 
Behavioral risk factors are not supported by current evidence as being primarily responsible for the rise in blood pressure among Black South Africans transitioning from optimal to hypertensive levels. Clinical focus remains on common underlying physiologic processes such as those seen in metabolic syndrome rather than isolated behavioral causation.
